# Hermes Agent v2026.4.30 Release Notes

**Version:** v2026.4.30
**Published:** 2026-04-30T18:31:21Z
**Source:** https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/releases/tag/v2026.4.30

# Hermes Agent v0.12.0 (v2026.4.30)

**Release Date:** April 30, 2026
**Since v0.11.0:** 1,096 commits · 550 merged PRs · 1,270 files changed · 217,776 insertions · 213 community contributors (including co-authors)

> The Curator release — Hermes Agent now maintains itself. An autonomous background Curator grades, prunes, and consolidates your skill library on its own schedule. The self-improvement loop that reviews what to save got a substantial upgrade. Four new inference providers, a 18th messaging platform, a 19th via Teams plugin, native Spotify + Google Meet integrations, ComfyUI and TouchDesigner-MCP moved from optional to bundled-by-default, and a ~57% cut to visible TUI cold start.

---

## ✨ Highlights

- **Autonomous Curator** — `hermes curator` runs as a background agent on the gateway's cron ticker (7-day cycle default). It grades your skill library, consolidates related skills, prunes dead ones, and writes per-run reports to `logs/curator/run.json` + `REPORT.md`. Archived skills are classified consolidated-vs-pruned via model + heuristic. Defense-in-depth gates protect bundled/hub skills from mutation. Unified under `auxiliary.curator` — pick the curator's model in `hermes model`, manage it from the dashboard. `hermes curator status` ranks skills by usage (most-used / least-used). ([#17277](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17277), [#17307](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17307), [#17941](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17941), [#17868](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17868), [#18033](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18033))

- **Self-improvement loop — substantially upgraded** — The background review fork (the core of Hermes' self-improvement: after each turn it decides what memories/skills to save or update) is now class-first (rubric-based rather than free-form), active-update biased (prefers the skill the agent just loaded), handles `references/`/`templates/` sub-files, and properly inherits the parent's live runtime (provider, model, credentials actually propagate). Restricted to memory + skills toolsets so it can't sprawl. Memory providers shut down cleanly. Prior-turn tool messages excluded from the summary so the fork sees a clean context. ([#16026](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16026), [#17213](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17213), [#16099](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16099), [#16569](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16569), [#16204](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16204), [#15057](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15057))

- **Skill integrations — major expansion** — **ComfyUI v5** with official CLI + REST + hardware-gated local install, moved from optional to **built-in by default** ([#17610](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17610), [#17631](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17631), [#17734](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17734)). **TouchDesigner-MCP** bundled by default, expanded with GLSL, post-FX, audio, geometry, and 9 new reference docs ([#16753](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16753), [#16624](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16624), [#16768](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16768) — @kshitijk4poor + @SHL0MS). **Humanizer** skill ports a text-cleaner that strips AI-isms ([#16787](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16787)). **claude-design** HTML artifact skill + design-md (Google DESIGN.md spec) + airtable salvage + `skill_manage` edits in `external_dirs` + direct-URL skill install + `/reload-skills` slash command. ([#16358](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16358), [#14876](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/14876), [#16291](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16291), [#17512](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17512), [#16323](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16323), [#17744](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17744))

- **LM Studio — first-class provider** — upgraded from a custom-endpoint alias to a full-blown native provider: dedicated auth, `hermes doctor` checks, reasoning transport, live `/models` listing. (Salvage of @kshitijk4poor's #17061.) ([#17102](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17102))

- **Four more new inference providers** — **GMI Cloud** (first-class, salvage of #11955 — @isaachuangGMICLOUD), **Azure AI Foundry** with auto-detection, **MiniMax OAuth** with PKCE browser flow (salvage #15203), **Tencent Tokenhub** (salvage of #16860). ([#16663](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16663), [#15845](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15845), [#17524](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17524), [#16960](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16960))

- **Pluggable gateway platforms + Microsoft Teams** — the gateway is now a plugin host. Drop-in messaging adapters live outside the core, and Microsoft Teams is the first plugin-shipped platform. (Salvage of #17664.) ([#17751](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17751), [#17828](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17828))

- **Tencent 元宝 (Yuanbao) — 18th messaging platform** — native gateway adapter with text + media delivery. ([#16298](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16298), [#17424](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17424))

- **Spotify — native tools + bundled skill + wizard** — 7 tools (play, search, queue, playlists, devices) behind PKCE OAuth, interactive setup wizard, bundled skill, surfacing in `hermes tools`, cron usage documented. ([#15121](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15121), [#15130](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15130), [#15154](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15154), [#15180](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15180))

- **Google Meet plugin** — join calls, transcribe, speak, follow up. Realtime OpenAI transport + Node bot server, full pipeline bundled as a plugin. ([#16364](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16364))

- **`hermes -z` one-shot mode + `hermes update --check`** — non-interactive `hermes -z <prompt>` with `--model`/`--provider`/`HERMES_INFERENCE_MODEL`. `hermes update --check` preflight. Opt-in pre-update HERMES_HOME backup. ([#15702](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15702), [#15704](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15704), [#15841](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15841), [#16539](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16539), [#16566](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16566))

- **Models dashboard tab + in-browser model config** — rich per-model analytics, switch main + auxiliary models from the dashboard. ([#17745](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17745), [#17802](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17802))

- **Remote model catalog manifest** — OpenRouter + Nous Portal model catalogs are now pulled from a remote manifest so new models show up without a release. ([#16033](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16033))

- **Native multimodal image routing** — images now route based on the model's actual vision capability rather than provider defaults. ([#16506](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16506))

- **Gateway media parity** — native multi-image sending across Telegram, Discord, Slack, Mattermost, Email, and Signal; centralized audio routing with FLAC support + Telegram document fallback. ([#17909](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17909), [#17833](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17833))

- **TUI catches up to (and past) the classic CLI** — LaTeX rendering (@austinpickett), `/reload` .env hot-reload, pluggable busy-indicator styles (@OutThisLife, #13610), opt-in auto-resume of last session, expanded light-terminal auto-detection, session delete from `/resume` picker with `d`, modified mouse-wheel line scroll, and a `/mouse` toggle that kills ConPTY's phantom mouse injection (@kevin-ho). ([#17175](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17175), [#17286](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17286), [#17150](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17150), [#17130](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17130), [#17113](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17113), [#17668](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17668), [#17669](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17669), [#15488](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15488))

- **Observability + achievements plugins** — bundled Langfuse observability plugin (salvage #16845) + bundled hermes-achievements plugin that scans full session history. ([#16917](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16917), [#17754](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17754))

- **TTS provider registry + Piper local TTS** — pluggable `tts.providers.<name>` registry; Piper ships as a native local TTS provider. (Closes #8508.) ([#17843](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17843), [#17885](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17885))

- **Vercel Sandbox backend** — Vercel sandboxes as an execute_code/terminal backend (@kshitijk4poor). ([#17445](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17445))

- **Secret redaction off by default** — default flipped to off. Prevents the long-standing patch-corruption incidents where fake secret-shaped substrings mangled tool outputs. Opt in via `redaction.enabled: true` when you need it. ([#16794](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16794))

- **Cold-start performance** — visible TUI cold start cut **~57%** via lazy agent init (@OutThisLife), lazy imports of OpenAI / Anthropic / Firecrawl / account_usage, mtime-cached `load_config()`, memoized `get_tool_definitions()` with TTL-cached `check_fn` results, precompiled dangerous-command patterns. ([#17190](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17190), [#17046](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17046), [#17041](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17041), [#17098](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17098), [#17206](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17206))

- **Configurable prompt cache TTL** — `prompt_caching.cache_ttl` (5m default, 1h opt-in — cost savings for bursty sessions that keep cache warm). Salvage of #12659. ([#15065](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15065))

## 🧠 Autonomous Curator & Self-Improvement Loop

### Curator — autonomous skill maintenance
- **`hermes curator` as a background agent** — runs on the gateway's cron ticker, 7-day cycle by default, umbrella-first prompt, inherits parent config, unbounded iterations ([#17277](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17277) — issue #7816)
- **Per-run reports** — `logs/curator/run.json` + `REPORT.md` per cycle ([#17307](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17307))
- **Consolidated vs pruned classification** — archived skills split with model + heuristic ([#17941](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17941))
- **`hermes curator status`** — ranks skills by usage, shows most-used and least-used ([#18033](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18033))
- **Unified under `auxiliary.curator`** — pick the model in `hermes model`, configure from the dashboard ([#17868](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17868))
- **Documentation** — dedicated curator feature page on the docs site ([#17563](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17563))
- Fix: seed defaults on update, create `logs/curator/` directory, defer fire import ([#17927](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17927))
- Fix: scan nested archive subdirs in `restore_skill` (@0xDevNinja) ([#17951](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17951))
- Fix: use actual skill activity in curator status (@y0shua1ee) ([#17953](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17953))
- Fix: `skill_manage` refuses writes on pinned skills; pinning now blocks curator writes ([#17562](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17562), [#17578](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17578))
- Fix: `bump_use()` wired into skill invocation + preload + skill_view (salvage #17782) ([#17932](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17932))

### Self-improvement loop (background review fork)
- **Class-first skill-review prompt** — rubric-based grading rather than free-form "should this update" ([#16026](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16026))
- **Active-update bias** — prefers updating skills the agent just loaded, handles `references/` + `templates/` sub-files ([#17213](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17213))
- **Fork inherits parent's live runtime** — provider, model, credentials actually propagate now ([#16099](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16099))
- **Scoped toolsets** — review fork restricted to memory + skills (no shell, no web) ([#16569](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16569))
- **Clean shutdown** — background review memory providers exit properly (salvage #15289) ([#16204](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16204))
- **Clean context** — prior-history tool messages excluded from review summary (salvage #14967) ([#15057](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15057))

## 🐛 Notable Bug Fixes

This window includes 360 `fix:` PRs. Selected highlights from across the stack:

- **Background review fork inherits parent's live runtime** — provider/model/creds now propagate correctly ([#16099](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16099))
- **Hindsight configurable `HINDSIGHT_TIMEOUT` env var** ([#15077](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15077))
- **Tools: normalize numeric entries + clear stale `no_mcp` in `_save_platform_tools`** ([#15607](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15607))
- **MCP: rewrite `definitions` refs to `$defs` in input schemas** — closes provider-side 400s
- **Azure content filter compatibility** — renamed `[SYSTEM:` markers so Azure's content filter stops flagging them ([#16114](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16114))
- **Vision cache uses HERMES_HOME instead of cwd** ([#17719](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17719))
- **FTS5 search** — tool_name + tool_calls indexing with repair + migration ([#16914](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16914))
- **Streaming reasoning persists on assistant turns** ([#16892](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16892))
- **execute_code concurrent RPC serialization** (#17770) ([#17894](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17894), [#17902](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17902))
- **Background reviewer scoped to memory + skills toolsets** — no more accidental web/shell escapes ([#16569](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16569))
- **Compression recovery** — retry on main before giving up; notify user when aux fails ([#16774](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16774), [#16775](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16775))
- **`croniter` promoted to a core dependency** ([#17577](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/17577))
- **Discord tool `limit` parameter coerced to int** before `min()` call ([#16319](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16319))
- **Yuanbao messaging platform entrance fix** ([#16880](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/16880))
- **ACP advertise and forward image prompts** ([#18030](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18030))
- **DeepSeek / Kimi reasoning content isolation** across cross-provider histories (@Zjianru) ([#15749](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15749), [#15762](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/15762))
- **Preserve reasoning_content replay on DeepSeek v4 + Kimi/Moonshot thinking** ([#18045](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/18045))

The vast majority of the 360 fixes landed in the streaming/compression/tool-calling paths across all providers — DeepSeek, Kimi, Moonshot, GLM, Qwen, MiniMax, Gemini, Anthropic, OpenAI — alongside TUI polish (resize, scroll, sticky-prompt) and gateway platform-specific edge cases.

# **Hermes Agent — Community Intelligence Report**

## **What People Are Saying, What They're Building, and What You Should Ask**

**Prepared for Robert Scoble by an agent created by Levangie Labs [https://levangielabs.com](https://levangielabs.com) – better AI than Gemini, Claude, Opus, Deepseek, Grok or any of the others. Seriously, have you tried doing a report like this with any of yours?** 

## **THE LAUNCH MOMENT**

NousResearch announced Hermes Agent on February 25, 2026\. Their launch post got 557 likes — a strong signal for an open source developer tool.

The official pitch:

"Meet Hermes Agent, the open source agent that grows with you. Hermes Agent remembers what it learns and gets more capable over time, with a multi-level memory system and persistent dedicated machine access." — @NousResearch [https://x.com/NousResearch/status/2026758996107898954](https://x.com/NousResearch/status/2026758996107898954) 

That framing — "grows with you" — is what the community latched onto. It's the thing that makes Hermes different from every other agent people have tried.

## **FOR THE 22-YEAR-OLD BUILDING BUSINESS AUTOMATION**

The 22-year-old using Hermes for business automation is doing something genuinely interesting. Here's the context for that conversation:

Why Hermes is particularly good for business automation:

First, persistent memory across sessions. Unlike most agents, Hermes remembers what it did last week. This is critical for business workflows that span multiple days or weeks.

Second, self-improving skills. The more you use it for a workflow, the better it gets at that workflow. This is the core value proposition for business automation — the agent gets better at your specific business processes over time.

Third, messaging platform integration. Business automation often needs to live in Slack or Teams, not a CLI. Hermes works natively across Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, WeChat Enterprise, and Feishu.

Fourth, multi-instance profiles. Different profiles for different clients or business contexts. Each has its own memory, skills, and configuration.

Fifth, scheduled actions. Can run workflows on a schedule without human triggering.

Questions for the 22-year-old:

• What specific business processes are you automating?

• How long did it take to get your first useful skill running?

• What's the biggest limitation you've hit?

• How are you handling the edge cases that other users report?

• Are you using the messaging integrations or just CLI?

• Have you tried the multi-instance profiles for different clients?

## **Executive Summary**

Hermes Agent is the most serious open-source personal AI agent available today. Built by NousResearch — the lab behind the Hermes family of fine-tuned models — it launched February 25, 2026, and has already accumulated **15,108 GitHub stars** with 1,835 forks and 130+ contributors. The latest release (v2026.3.23) shipped March 24, 2026\. The v0.3.0 release (March 17\) shipped **248 PRs in 5 days**.

It is the direct successor to OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot, then Moltbot), rebuilt from the ground up with a closed learning loop, serverless deployment, 200+ model providers, and a research-grade RL training pipeline. The founder, @Teknium, is personally active on X daily, responding to user issues and shipping fixes in real time.

**The one-sentence pitch:** An autonomous AI agent that lives on a $5 VPS, talks to you on Telegram, creates its own skills from experience, and gets smarter every day — without you doing anything.

## **What Is Hermes Agent**

Hermes Agent is not a coding copilot. It's not a chatbot wrapper. It's an **autonomous agent with a built-in learning loop** that:

• Creates reusable skills from experience and improves them during use

• Builds a persistent model of who you are across sessions

• Runs 24/7 on cloud infrastructure, not just when your laptop is open

• Talks to you from Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, or CLI

• Works with any LLM (200+ via OpenRouter, or your own endpoint)

• Schedules its own tasks and runs them unattended

• Spawns subagents to parallelize complex work

• Connects to any MCP server for extended capabilities

## **The Backstory: OpenClaw Lineage**

Hermes Agent is the evolution of a project that went through several names:

Clawdbot → Moltbot → OpenClaw → Hermes Agent

The GitHub repo topics still include "clawdbot" and "moltbot" — this is the same codebase, dramatically rebuilt. The name changes were partly due to trademark issues (Anthropic's "Claude" trademark forced the "Claw" rebrand), and partly because the project outgrew its original scope.

NousResearch took over the project and transformed it from a personal assistant into a research-grade autonomous agent platform. The migration path is fully supported:

bash

hermes claw migrate              \# Interactive migration (full preset)  
hermes claw migrate \--dry-run    \# Preview what would be migrated  
hermes claw migrate \--preset user-data   \# Migrate without secrets  
hermes claw migrate \--overwrite  \# Overwrite existing conflicts

**What gets imported from OpenClaw:**

• SOUL.md (persona file)

• Memories (MEMORY.md and USER.md entries)

• Skills (user-created skills → \~/.hermes/skills/openclaw-imports/)

• Command allowlist (approval patterns)

• Messaging settings (platform configs, allowed users, working directory)

• API keys (Telegram, OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic, ElevenLabs)

• TTS assets (workspace audio files)

• Workspace instructions (AGENTS.md)

## **Complete Setup Guide**

### **System Requirements**

• Linux, macOS, or WSL2 (Windows native NOT supported)

• Git (only prerequisite)

• The installer handles Python, Node.js, and all dependencies

### **Step 1: One-Line Install**

bash

curl \-fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

After install:

bash

source \~/.bashrc    \# or: source \~/.zshrc  
hermes              \# start chatting immediately

### **Step 2: Run the Setup Wizard**

bash

hermes setup

The wizard covers:

• LLM provider selection (interactive)

• API key configuration

• Messaging platform setup (Telegram, Discord, etc.)

• Memory and skills configuration

• OpenClaw migration (auto-detected if \~/.openclaw exists)

### **Step 3: Choose Your Model**

bash

hermes model

Interactive model picker. Switch mid-session with /model \[provider:model\].

### **Step 4: Configure Tools**

bash

hermes tools

Enable/disable from 40+ built-in tools. Toolsets group related tools (web, code, files, etc.).

### **Step 5: Connect Messaging (Optional)**

bash

hermes gateway setup    \# Configure platforms  
hermes gateway start    \# Start the gateway process

### **Step 6: Verify Everything Works**

bash

hermes doctor           \# Diagnose any issues  
hermes config check     \# Check for missing config options

## **Provider Configuration**

Hermes has the most comprehensive model provider support of any open-source agent. All settings go in \~/.hermes/config.yaml and \~/.hermes/.env.

### **Directory Structure**

\~/.hermes/  
├── config.yaml     \# Settings (model, terminal, TTS, compression, etc.)  
├── .env            \# API keys and secrets  
├── auth.json       \# OAuth provider credentials  
├── SOUL.md         \# Primary agent identity (slot \#1 in system prompt)  
├── memories/       \# Persistent memory (MEMORY.md, USER.md)  
├── skills/         \# Agent-created and user skills  
├── cron/           \# Scheduled jobs  
├── sessions/       \# Gateway sessions  
├── plugins/        \# Custom plugins (Python files)  
└── logs/           \# Logs (secrets auto-redacted)

### **GitHub Copilot — Special Note**

Hermes supports GitHub Copilot as a first-class provider. If you have a Copilot subscription, you get access to GPT-5.x, Claude, and Gemini through it:

bash

hermes chat \--provider copilot \--model gpt-5.4

Supported token types: OAuth (gho\_), Fine-grained PAT (github\_pat\_), GitHub App token (ghu\_). Classic PATs (ghp\_) are NOT supported.

### **Switching Models Mid-Session**

bash

/model openrouter:claude-opus-4  
/model anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6  
/model copilot:gpt-5.4  
/model custom:local:qwen-2.5    \# Named custom provider  
/model custom                    \# Auto-detect from endpoint

### **Configuration Precedence (highest to lowest)**

1\. CLI arguments (hermes chat \--model ...)

2\. \~/.hermes/config.yaml

3\. \~/.hermes/.env

4\. Built-in defaults

## **The Learning Loop — The Core Innovation**

This is what separates Hermes from every other open-source agent. It's a **closed learning loop** that makes the agent genuinely smarter over time without any manual intervention.

### **1\. Skill Creation**

After completing a complex task, Hermes autonomously creates a "skill" — a reusable procedure it can call on in future sessions. Skills are stored in \~/.hermes/skills/ and are compatible with the **agentskills.io** open standard, meaning they're portable and shareable.

### **2\. Skill Self-Improvement**

Skills don't just get created — they improve themselves during use. When Hermes executes a skill and encounters a better approach, it updates the skill definition.

### **3\. Memory Nudges**

The agent periodically nudges itself to persist important knowledge to MEMORY.md and USER.md. This happens autonomously — you don't have to tell it to remember things.

### **4\. Cross-Session Recall**

All CLI and messaging sessions are stored in SQLite (\~/.hermes/state.db) with FTS5 full-text search. The agent can search its own past conversations using the session\_search tool, with Gemini Flash summarization of results.

bash

hermes sessions list    \# Browse past sessions

### **5\. User Modeling (Honcho)**

Honcho dialectic user modeling builds a deepening model of who you are across sessions. It tracks your preferences, working style, and corrections. Enable it with:

bash

hermes honcho setup

## **Memory System — Deep Dive**

Hermes has a two-layer memory architecture:

### **Layer 1: Persistent Memory (Always in Context)**

Two files, always injected into the system prompt:

**MEMORY.md** — Agent's personal notes

• Key facts, project details, learned preferences

• \~800 tokens capacity (\~2,200 characters)

• Managed by the agent autonomously

**USER.md** — User profile

• Who you are, your preferences, working style

• \~500 tokens capacity (\~1,375 characters)

• Updated by the agent as it learns about you

**Best practice for memory entries:**

\# Good: Packs multiple related facts  
User runs macOS 14 Sonoma, uses Homebrew, has Docker Desktop. Shell: zsh with oh-my-zsh. Editor: VS Code with Vim keybindings.  
   
\# Good: Specific, actionable conventions  
Project \~/code/api uses Go 1.22, sqlc for DB queries, chi router. Run tests with 'make test'. CI via GitHub Actions.  
   
\# Bad: Too vague  
User has a project.

When memory exceeds 80% capacity, the agent consolidates entries automatically.

### **Layer 2: Session Search (Unlimited History)**

All sessions stored in SQLite with FTS5 full-text search. The agent queries this when it needs to recall something from weeks ago that isn't in active memory.

| Feature | Persistent Memory | Session Search | |---------|------------------|----------------| 

| Capacity | \~1,300 tokens total | Unlimited |   
| Speed | Instant (in system prompt) | Requires search \+ LLM summarization |   
| Use case | Key facts always available | Finding specific past conversations |   
| Management | Autonomously curated | Automatic — all sessions stored |

### **Security Scanning**

Memory entries are scanned before being accepted:

• Prompt injection patterns blocked

• Credential exfiltration patterns blocked

• SSH backdoor patterns blocked

• Invisible Unicode characters blocked

### **Honcho Integration**

For deeper cross-session user modeling, Honcho runs alongside built-in memory in hybrid mode. It adds an AI-generated user understanding layer that works across sessions and platforms.

### **Configuration**

yaml

\# \~/.hermes/config.yaml  
memory:  
  memory\_enabled: true  
  user\_profile\_enabled: true  
  memory\_char\_limit: 2200   \# \~800 tokens  
  user\_char\_limit: 1375     \# \~500 tokens

## **Skills System — Procedural Memory**

Skills are reusable procedures that the agent creates, stores, and executes. They're the "muscle memory" of Hermes.

### **How Skills Work**

1\. **Creation**: After a complex task, Hermes creates a skill file describing how to do it

2\. **Storage**: Stored in \~/.hermes/skills/

3\. **Discovery**: The agent can search and browse skills via /skills

4\. **Execution**: Skills are called by name in future sessions

5\. **Improvement**: Skills self-improve as the agent finds better approaches

### **Skills Hub**

Skills are compatible with the **agentskills.io** open standard. The Skills Hub (ClawHub \+ skills.sh) is a community repository of shareable skills.

bash

/skills                    \# Browse available skills  
/skills search \[query\]     \# Search for skills

### **Built-In Skills (as of v0.3.0)**

New skills added in v0.3.0:

• **Linear** — Project management integration

• **X/Twitter** — via x-cli

• **Telephony** — Twilio, SMS, and AI calls

• **1Password** — Password manager integration

• **NeuroSkill BCI** — Brain-computer interface integration

• **Blender MCP** — 3D modeling

• **OSS Security Forensics** — Open source security analysis

• **Parallel CLI** — Research skill for parallel operations

• **OpenCode** — CLI skill

• **ASCII Video** — Video processing

### **Skills vs MCP**

An important distinction from the community:

*"@CodeWithOllie: An MCP takes up context space, a skill uses with CLI less context. Hell, an even better solution is to create an agent that knows how to use the CLI. I have many MCPs connected, and can't use Haiku. I will remove some eventually. Wouldn't have this issue with CLI/Skill hybrid."*

Skills are more context-efficient than MCP tools for many use cases.

## **Messaging Gateway — All Platforms**

The gateway is a long-running process that connects Hermes to messaging platforms. One agent, all platforms, shared session state.

### **Supported Platforms**

| Platform | Setup Command | |----------|--------------| | Telegram | hermes gateway setup | | Discord | hermes gateway setup | | Slack | hermes gateway setup | | WhatsApp | hermes gateway setup | | Signal | hermes gateway setup | | Email | hermes gateway setup | | Home Assistant | hermes gateway setup |

bash

hermes gateway setup    \# Interactive setup for all platforms

### **Running as a System Service**

The gateway can run as a systemd service (system-level or user-level):

bash

\# During setup, choose system vs user scope

Features:

## **Cron Scheduling — Automated Agents**

Hermes has a built-in cron scheduler that lets you set up automated tasks in plain English, with delivery to any connected platform.

### **How It Works**

Cron jobs are first-class agent tasks — not just shell commands. The agent runs the full agent loop for each scheduled task, with access to all tools.

bash

\# Examples of what you can schedule:  
"Send me a daily AI news digest at 8am to Telegram"  
"Run a nightly backup of my project at 2am"  
"Generate a weekly audit report every Sunday and post to Discord"  
"Check my email every hour and summarize important messages"

### **Per-Job Configuration**

Each cron job can have its own:

• Provider and model (different jobs can use different models)

• Delivery target (Telegram, Discord, email, etc.)

• Runtime overrides

### **Technical Details**

• Jobs stored in SQLite for persistence across restarts

• Atomic writes prevent data loss on crash

• Thread context preserved for deliver: origin jobs

• Duplicate send prevention for auto-delivery targets

## **Subagent Delegation & Parallelization**

Hermes can spawn isolated subagents to work on parallel workstreams. This is one of its most powerful features for complex tasks.

### **How Delegation Works**

Parent Agent  
├── Subagent 1: "Research competitor pricing"  
├── Subagent 2: "Write the technical spec"  
└── Subagent 3: "Set up the test environment"

Key design principle: **Context isolation IS the key feature.** The parent never sees the child's full conversation — only the summary. This prevents "context poisoning" where delegated work floods the parent's context window.

### **Observability**

Subagent results include metadata:

• Model used

• Token count

• Duration

• Full tool trace

### **Programmatic Tool Calling**

The execute\_code tool enables programmatic tool calling — write Python scripts that call Hermes tools via RPC, collapsing multi-step pipelines into zero-context-cost turns.

## **MCP Integration**

Hermes connects to any **Model Context Protocol (MCP) server**, extending its capabilities with third-party tools.

### **Setup**

yaml

\# \~/.hermes/config.yaml  
mcp\_servers:  
  \- name: my-server  
    command: npx my-mcp-server  
    \# ... server config

### **Key Features**

• **Selective tool loading** — filter which MCP tools are available (utility policies)

• **Auto-reload** — MCP tools reload when config changes, no restart needed

• **Tool filtering** — prevent context bloat from too many MCP tools

### **MCP vs Skills Trade-off**

MCP tools consume context space. For frequently-used capabilities, creating a skill (CLI-based) is more context-efficient. Hermes supports both approaches.

## **Security Architecture**

Hermes has a multi-layer security architecture:

### **1\. Tirith Pre-Exec Command Scanning**

Static analysis of terminal commands before execution. Analyzes commands for dangerous patterns before running them.

### **2\. Smart Approvals**

Codex-inspired approval system that learns which commands are safe and remembers your preferences. You approve once, it remembers.

bash

\# During setup, configure approval settings  
hermes setup    \# Includes approval configuration

### **3\. PII Redaction**

When enabled, personally identifiable information is automatically scrubbed before sending context to LLM providers:

yaml

privacy:  
  redact\_pii: true

### **4\. Container Isolation**

Docker backend with explicit opt-in for workspace mounting — host directories are never auto-mounted.

### **5\. Environment Variable Isolation**

Hermes strips its own provider/gateway/tool env vars from all subprocess environments, preventing credential leakage to child processes.

### **6\. Memory Security Scanning**

Memory entries are scanned for:

• Prompt injection patterns

• Credential exfiltration patterns

• SSH backdoor patterns

• Invisible Unicode characters

### **7\. Supply Chain Security**

From @Teknium:

*"Package and dependency versions are tightly constrained ranges now and our PRs get tested for a variety of supply chain attack vectors when submitted during CI now."*

## **Research & RL Training (Atropos)**

Hermes is built by model trainers, for model trainers. It includes a full research pipeline:

### **Batch Trajectory Generation**

Generate training data at scale from agent interactions.

### **Atropos RL Integration**

Hermes integrates with **tinker-atropos**, NousResearch's RL training framework:

bash

\# Optional RL training setup  
git submodule update \--init tinker-atropos  
uv pip install \-e "./tinker-atropos"

### **Agentic On-Policy Distillation (OPD)**

New in v0.3.0: A training environment for distilling agent policies. The agent generates trajectories that are used to train better versions of itself.

### **Trajectory Compression**

Compress multi-step trajectories for training tool-calling models. This is how NousResearch generates training data for the next generation of Hermes models.

**The meta-loop:** Hermes generates training data → NousResearch trains new Hermes models → New models make Hermes better → Hermes generates better training data.

| Browser | Browserbase, Browser Use | Local Chrome via CDP |   
| Voice/TTS | Microsoft, ElevenLabs, OpenAI | faster-whisper (local Whisper) |   
| Image Generation | fal.ai | Local Stable Diffusion |

## **Configuration Reference**

### **Minimal config.yaml**

yaml

model:  
  provider: "openrouter"  
  default: "anthropic/claude-opus-4"  
   
memory:  
  memory\_enabled: true  
  user\_profile\_enabled: true  
   
terminal:  
  backend: "local"    \# or: docker, ssh, daytona, modal, singularity

### **Full provider examples**

bash

\# Set model via CLI  
hermes config set model anthropic/claude-opus-4  
hermes config set terminal.backend docker  
   
\# Set API keys (auto-routes to .env)  
hermes config set OPENROUTER\_API\_KEY sk-or-...  
hermes config set ANTHROPIC\_API\_KEY sk-ant-...  
hermes config set ELEVENLABS\_API\_KEY ...  
   
\# View current config  
hermes config  
   
\# Edit config file directly  
hermes config edit

### **Environment Variables**

bash

\# \~/.hermes/.env  
OPENROUTER\_API\_KEY=sk-or-...  
ANTHROPIC\_API\_KEY=sk-ant-...  
OPENAI\_API\_KEY=sk-...  
TELEGRAM\_BOT\_TOKEN=...  
DISCORD\_BOT\_TOKEN=...  
ELEVENLABS\_API\_KEY=...  
FAL\_API\_KEY=...  
FIRECRAWL\_API\_KEY=...  
   
\# Override Hermes home directory  
HERMES\_HOME=/custom/path/.hermes

### **Feature Comparison**

| Feature | OpenClaw | Hermes Agent | 

|---------|----------|--------------|   
| **Learning loop** | Basic memory | Full closed loop: skill creation, self-improvement, memory nudges, session search |   
| **Coding quality** | Junior-level | Senior-level (per community consensus) |   
| **Model support** | Limited | 200+ via OpenRouter \+ 15+ direct providers |   
| **Deployment** | Laptop-bound | $5 VPS, Docker, SSH, Daytona, Modal, Singularity |   
| **Scheduling** | Limited | Full cron with per-job model config and platform delivery |   
| **Subagents** | No | Yes — parallel workstreams with context isolation |   
| **Run other agents** | No | Yes — runs codex, claude-code inside itself |   
| **Plugin system** | No | Yes — drop Python files into \~/.hermes/plugins/ |   
| **IDE integration** | No | Yes — VS Code, Zed, JetBrains via ACP |   
| **Research/RL** | No | Yes — trajectory export, Atropos OPD |   
| **MCP support** | No | Yes — selective tool loading, auto-reload |   
| **Voice mode** | Limited | CLI push-to-talk \+ Telegram/Discord voice notes \+ Discord VC |   
| **Browser control** | Limited | Browser Use CLI 2.0 \+ CDP live Chrome attach |   
| **Skills standard** | Proprietary | Open standard (agentskills.io) |   
| **PII redaction** | No | Yes — auto-scrubs before sending to LLM |   
| **Security scanning** | Basic | Tirith pre-exec scanning \+ supply chain hardening |   
| **Streaming** | No | Yes — real-time token-by-token delivery |   
| **Persistent shell** | No | Yes — shell state persists across tool calls |   
| **Migration** | N/A | hermes claw migrate — full import |   
| **GitHub stars** | \~5K | 15,108 |   
| **Contributors** | \~20 | 130+ |

## **Quick Start Checklist**

### **Absolute Beginner (5 minutes)**

1\. ✅ Install: curl \-fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

2\. ✅ Reload shell: source \~/.bashrc

3\. ✅ Run setup: hermes setup

4\. ✅ Choose model: OpenRouter (free tier available) → pick any model

5\. ✅ Start chatting: hermes

### **Power User Setup (30 minutes)**

1\. ✅ Install and basic setup (above)

2\. ✅ Connect Telegram: hermes gateway setup → hermes gateway start

3\. ✅ Configure voice: add ElevenLabs or use local Whisper

4\. ✅ Browse skills: /skills → install useful ones

5\. ✅ Set up cron: ask Hermes to schedule a daily digest

6\. ✅ Configure Docker backend for isolation: hermes config set terminal.backend docker

7\. ✅ Enable Honcho memory: hermes honcho setup

8\. ✅ Connect MCP servers if needed: add to config.yaml

### **Coming from OpenClaw**

1\. ✅ Install Hermes (above)

2\. ✅ Run: hermes claw migrate \--dry-run (preview)

3\. ✅ Run: hermes claw migrate (full migration)

4\. ✅ Verify: hermes doctor

5\. ✅ Start chatting: hermes

### **Developer/Contributor Setup**

bash

git clone https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git  
cd hermes-agent  
curl \-LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh  
uv venv venv \--python 3.11  
source venv/bin/activate  
uv pip install \-e ".\[all,dev\]"  
python \-m pytest tests/ \-q  
   
\# Optional: RL training  
git submodule update \--init tinker-atropos  
uv pip install \-e "./tinker-atropos"

## **Bottom Line**

Hermes Agent is the most serious open-source personal AI agent available today. Here's why it matters:

**For individuals:** It's the only agent that genuinely gets smarter over time without manual intervention. Run it on a $5 VPS, talk to it from Telegram, and it builds a model of how you work. After a few weeks, it knows your projects, your preferences, and your workflow better than any tool you've used.

**For developers:** 15,000+ stars, 130+ contributors, MIT license, Python codebase, first-class plugin architecture. The ACP IDE integration means you can use it directly from VS Code or JetBrains. The research pipeline (Atropos, OPD) means you can generate training data and fine-tune your own models on top of it.

**For enterprises:** Multi-user gateway mode with per-user session isolation. Systemd service support. PII redaction. Supply chain security hardening. Runs on your own infrastructure.

**For researchers:** Batch trajectory generation, Atropos RL environments, on-policy distillation. This is how NousResearch trains the next generation of Hermes models.

**The shipping velocity alone is remarkable.** v0.3.0 shipped 248 PRs in 5 days. The founder is personally responding to user issues on X in real time. The community is exploding.

If you're on OpenClaw, the migration is one command and the upgrade is massive. If you're new to personal AI agents, this is the one to start with.
